race.name: Šumavský Skimaraton
race.tagline: Forty-six kilometers through the ancient Šumava forests at the heart of Bohemia — where Central Europe's last primeval wilderness hides a demanding freestyle marathon above the clouds.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations through the national park course

climate.description: February at 1,000m+ in the Šumava mountains means reliable winter conditions. The Bohemian Forest is one of the snowiest regions in Central Europe. Temperatures typically -8°C to -1°C. Being further south than Scandinavian races, conditions can be more variable.
challenges: Central European snow reliability less certain than Scandinavia
Altitude-dependent conditions — lower sections may be marginal
Wind on exposed mountain plateau sections
National park terrain includes some challenging descents

course.primary: Starting from Kvilda, one of the highest villages in the Czech Republic, the course traverses the Šumava National Park plateau. The terrain features rolling mountain forest with open moorland sections. Šumava (Bohemian Forest) contains some of Europe's last primeval forests and is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.
course.surface: Machine-groomed freestyle tracks through national park terrain
features: Races through Šumava National Park — UNESCO Biosphere Reserve
Kvilda start at 1,065m — one of Czech Republic's highest villages
Ancient primeval forest sections of the Bohemian Forest
Open moorland and mountain plateau terrain

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Well-rounded race with no major weaknesses. Scenery lifted by UNESCO Biosphere Reserve status and ancient forest. Good field of 1,500 for a Central European race. 40-year history and Euroloppet membership provide solid credibility. Snow reliability is the main risk factor.

history.summary: Founded in 1984, the Šumavský Skimaraton is a 46km freestyle race through Šumava National Park in the Bohemian Forest of South Bohemia, Czech Republic. Part of the Euroloppet series, it starts from Kvilda, one of the highest and coldest villages in the country. The Šumava range straddles the Czech-German-Austrian border and contains some of Europe's last remaining primeval forest.
notable_facts: Šumava National Park is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve
Kvilda is one of the coldest places in the Czech Republic — extreme temperature records below -40°C
The Bohemian Forest (Šumava/Böhmerwald) spans Czech, German, and Austrian territory
Contains some of Europe's last primeval old-growth forest
Founded in 1984, over 40 years of racing history

videos.title: Z ORLICKÝCH HOR DO JESENÍKŮ - nonstop na běžkách - 200 km / 5000 m
videos.description:
Rozhodl jsem se vyzkoušet bezpečné cestování mezi kraji. V neděli 14.2.2021 před polednem jsem vyrazil na delší a svižnější "výlet" na běžkách. Myšlenku na tento přechod jsem měl v hlavě už několik let a právě teď nadešel ten správný čas. Tolik sněhu totiž už dlouho nebylo…
Vystartoval jsem z Deštné v Orlických horách na trasu dlouhou 200 km nonstop s převýšením téměř 5000 m a po třiceti hodinách dorazil do Karlové Studánky a tam padnul únavou.
To celé sám, bez podpory s velkou svačinou, lyžema, batohem a dobrou náladou... 

Tomáš Petreček
Můj život je jedno velké dobrodružství...

Sledovat ho můžete zde:

videos.title: Trénink Skiraplat - 5.3.2013
videos.description: Trénink našich nejmenších. Již skoro jarní výlet z Vodníka údolím křesánovského potoka na louky pod Drozdárnu. Krásné lyžování přímo ve Vimperku, nádherné počasí a nezapomenutelné zážitky :-)
